name: Computerphile
oneLine: Long-running British educational channel where University of Nottingham academics explain CS topics on camera.
bio: Computerphile is the computer-science sister channel to Numberphile, created by filmmaker Brady Haran and produced primarily by Sean Riley since April 2013. Episodes are interview-style: a University of Nottingham (or visiting) academic such as Mike Pound, David Brailsford, or Steve Bagley explains a CS topic - cryptography, algorithms, AI safety, compilers - usually with marker-and-paper diagrams. As of 2026 the channel is past 2.6M subscribers and 232M views.
style: Single-camera interview-style explainers, 6-20 minutes, almost always with the academic drawing on a paper pad.
notable: AI safety series with Robert Miles
